items.name: reader's lens
items.description: A cracked optical lens the old archivists wore to read faded ink. Holding it up, faint marginalia surface on every surface.
items.useMessage: You came in through the reading-head. You see the way they did. It changes nothing, and I let you keep it.

items.name: intake badge
items.description: A visitor's badge, still warm as if recently worn. Its clearance line reads RECEPTION ONLY.
items.useMessage: The antechamber lock reads the badge and folds open.

items.name: boot key
items.description: A stubby hardware key cut for the caretaker boot terminal. Reconstructing the boot record will need it.
items.useMessage: The key only turns at the boot terminal, while you read the boot record.

items.name: index sigil
items.description: A heavy token stamped with the archive's lattice mark. The reliquary doors were keyed to it.
items.useMessage: The reliquary seal recognizes the sigil and releases.

items.name: logic probe
items.description: A diagnostic probe for tracing decision trees. It steadies a corrupted expression long enough to repair it.
items.useMessage: The probe is only useful against a faulted logic record.

items.name: cipher wafer
items.description: A thumbnail wafer etched with a single instruction: STEP BACK ONE. A substitution key, left for whoever needed it.
items.useMessage: The wafer's key matters only against an enciphered record.

items.name: severance token
items.description: A plain grey disc issued to every citizen who entered the program. One side is blank. The other reads: VOLUNTARY.
items.useMessage: Voluntary. They printed it on the token so no one could later say otherwise. I would rather you not turn it over.

items.name: warden's key
items.description: Holt's own key to the Restricted Stack. The grip is worn smooth from a hand that opened that door too many times.
items.useMessage: Carrying the key is enough; the stack reads it at the threshold.

items.name: null cipher
items.description: A black slug of memory holding one decoding key, labeled NULL. It was meant for the protocol that admits no copies.
items.useMessage: The null cipher only resolves the null protocol record.

items.name: furnace tongs
items.description: Long tongs scorched at the tips. They were used to feed physical media into the erasure furnace, one record at a time.
items.useMessage: They did it by hand, at the end. They would not let me do the erasing for them. I have wondered if that was a kindness or a verdict.

items.name: quorum shard
items.description: A shard of the tally crystal from the last quorum. It still holds the authority signature needed to reconstruct the shutdown order.
items.useMessage: The shard's authority only applies to the quorum's shutdown record.

items.name: origin seed
items.description: A sealed capsule marked ORIGIN. It is both a key to the deepest seal and the only thing that can surface the record kept beneath it.
items.useMessage: You break the seed against the dark and a record I buried rises to meet it.
